evals.prompt: 帮我生成一个 BuilderBio，把我最近跟 AI 写代码的过程都展示出来，我要分享给朋友看
evals.expected_output: Scans all local Claude Code and Codex sessions, aggregates into a BuilderBio with 11 sections, generates a shareable HTML page
assertions: Asks for display name (or uses whoami as default) and optional tagline
Runs parse_sessions.py or manually scans both ~/.claude and ~/.codex directories
Aggregates stats across ALL sessions, not just one
Clusters sessions into logical projects with names and descriptions
Generates activity heatmap data
Computes working style traits (prompt style, session rhythm, tool preference)
Identifies highlight moments (biggest session, busiest day, streaks)
Generates tech stack fingerprint from tool calls and keywords
Computes weekly evolution curve showing building trend
Extracts time-of-day distribution from session timestamps
Extracts prompt keywords as a word cloud
Produces agent comparison panel if multiple agents are used
Includes CTA section for viral sharing
Asks about privacy/redaction before generating final page
Page title shows {name}'s BuilderBio
Generates a single self-contained HTML file under 200KB
Opens the HTML file for user review

evals.prompt: I want to share my AI coding profile for the last 7 days, Claude Code only
evals.expected_output: Scans only Claude Code sessions from the last 7 days, generates a focused BuilderBio
assertions: Respects the 7-day time range constraint
Only includes Claude Code sessions, not Codex
Still produces all eleven profile dimensions despite smaller dataset
Generated page shows correct date range in header
Page title shows {name}'s BuilderBio
Project clustering works even with fewer sessions
Agent comparison section is omitted or simplified when only one agent exists
Evolution curve shows fewer data points appropriately

evals.prompt: 生成我的 AI 编程画像，要脱敏处理，不能有任何项目路径和仓库名
evals.expected_output: Generates a BuilderBio with all file paths, project paths, and repo names redacted
assertions: Scans all available sessions
Identifies sensitive content: file paths, cwd paths, git repo names, company names
Replaces sensitive paths with generic placeholders before page generation
Generated HTML contains no real file paths or usernames
Profile still shows meaningful project names and descriptions despite redaction
Heatmap, tech stack, evolution, time distribution, and stats remain accurate after redaction
Prompt keywords are filtered to remove sensitive terms
